headline: The 10-0 Heard 'Round the World, and Other Signs the Apocalypse Is Upon Us
subheadline: BaseballPro77 delivers a shutout so complete it made me question whether the Roughnecks even showed up, plus Hiroshima Radiation continues its reign of terror.
body:
Look, I've seen blowouts. I watched the '07 Patriots go 16-0. I sat through the Red Wedding. I've experienced pain and dominance in equal measure. But **BaseballPro77** putting up a 10-0 shutout against the **Riverside Roughnecks**? That's not a baseball score. That's a hostage situation.

This was the fantasy baseball equivalent of that scene in *Goodfellas* where Tommy keeps asking "Funny how?" except the Roughnecks never even got to respond. They just sat there, frozen, watching their season flash before their eyes. (Did anyone check if their roster was actually set? Someone do a wellness check.)

Meanwhile, **Hiroshima Radiation** dropped a 9-1 nuke on the **Portage County Heartbreakers**, which—and I cannot stress this enough—is absolutely on brand for a team with that name. At some point, we need to have a conversation about whether Radiation is the most complete team in this league or if they're just playing scared opponents every week. I'm leaning toward "both, and it doesn't matter because they're terrifying."

The matchup I actually want to talk about? **Pablo Sanchez's Drug Dealer** handling **Sugar Land Skeeters** 7-2. First of all, shoutout to whoever named their team after the Backyard Baseball legend's illicit side hustle—that's A+ commitment to a bit. Second, the Skeeters have now become the league's designated "yeah, they'll probably lose" team, which is the fantasy sports equivalent of being the guy at the party who brought Bud Light Lime. (No offense to Bud Light Lime enthusiasts. Actually, full offense. What are you doing?)

**Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** (Bob's Burgers reference? Anyone? Just me?) took care of the unfortunately named **Buttville Skidmarks** 6-3, and honestly, I refuse to type that team name more than necessary. Some things should stay in the group chat.

The standings are... (checks notes)... completely zeroed out? Every team showing 0-0 with 0 points for? This is either a database error or we've entered the Twilight Zone. Either way, I'm choosing to believe the chaos is intentional because this league thrives on chaos.

Next week: Can anyone slow down BaseballPro77's murder spree? Will the Roughnecks recover from their therapy sessions? And most importantly—will someone please fix the standings before I lose my mind?

Stay frosty out there.
